tasks.title: Infra - Supabase Storage bucket setup and misokinesia stimuli verification
tasks.summary: Provision the public misokinesia storage bucket and verify the seeded stimulus metadata for launch.
tasks.description: Set up the public Supabase Storage bucket `misokinesia-stimuli`, upload placeholder or final mp4 clips, and verify that the existing seed script populates one active test set plus 29 active stimuli rows with the expected storage paths, durations, and sort order. Confirm that the public CDN URLs resolve without authentication using the documented `{SUPABASE_URL}/storage/v1/object/public/misokinesia-stimuli/{storage_path}` pattern.
acceptance_criteria: `misokinesia-stimuli` Supabase Storage bucket exists with public access enabled
At least one placeholder or final mp4 is uploaded and its public URL is directly accessible in a browser with no auth
`backend/admin_cli/seed_misokinesia_stimuli.py` exists and can seed the misokinesia test set plus 29 stimuli rows
Running the seed inserts exactly 1 row in `misokinesia_test_sets` and 29 rows in `misokinesia_stimuli`
All 29 stimuli rows have correct `sort_order`, `storage_path`, and `duration_ms` values
POST `/misokinesia/start` returns manifest URLs that load immediately with no 403 or redirect
notes: The seed script already exists in the repo; the remaining work is external bucket provisioning, asset upload, and verification.

tasks.title: Analytics v2 - Contract scaffolding and version bump
tasks.summary: Define the backend/frontend analytics v2 contract for day-level temperature summaries before modeling or UI changes land.
tasks.description: Update shared analytics constants and typed response models to introduce the `dashboard-analytics-v2` / `weather-mlm-v2` contract plus a scaffolded `temperature_summary` payload. Limit this patch to constants, backend Pydantic schemas, frontend API typings, and schema-level tests. Do not change mixed-model logic, snapshot orchestration, or dashboard rendering in this task.
acceptance_criteria: `backend/app/analytics/constants.py` defines `ANALYTICS_RESPONSE_VERSION=\"dashboard-analytics-v2\"` and `ANALYTICS_MODEL_VERSION=\"weather-mlm-v2\"`
`backend/app/schemas/analytics.py` includes typed models for `temperature_summary`, summary windows, frequency bins, and hot/cold group payloads
`frontend/src/lib/api/index.ts` matches the new backend response shape including `temperature_summary`
`backend/tests/test_analytics_schema.py` is updated to serialize a valid scaffolded v2 payload
notes: Keep this task schema-only so later implementation tasks can build on a stable typed contract.
Do not wire `temperature_summary` into live responses yet.

tasks.title: Analytics v2 - Day-level weather z-score parity
tasks.summary: Refactor mixed-model preparation so day-level weather predictors are standardized by unique study day rather than repeated participant rows.
tasks.description: Change the analytics modeling path so `temperature`, `precipitation`, and `daylight_hours` are z-scored across unique `date_local` values in the complete-case sample for each outcome, then mapped back onto participant rows before fitting. Keep participant-level predictors and outcomes standardized on participant rows, and leave the mixed-model formulas unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: For each outcome, weather predictor z-scores are computed from unique complete-case study days only
`anxiety`, `depression`, `loneliness`, `self_report`, and `digit_span_score` continue to standardize across participant rows in the complete-case sample
Uneven participant counts per day no longer change the weather z-score assigned to a given day
Existing model formulas and random intercept grouping by `date_bin` remain unchanged
Regression tests cover repeated-day weighting and zero-variance warning behavior
notes: Do not add the temperature summary payload in this task.
Avoid changing response schemas or route-handler code here.

tasks.title: Analytics v2 - Temperature summary engine
tasks.summary: Build a standalone backend helper that computes overall and seasonal day-level temperature summaries, histograms, and hot/cold groups.
tasks.description: Add a dedicated analytics helper module for `temperature_summary` that works from the canonical analytics dataset rows without changing the mixed-model path. The helper should generate `overall`, `fall_winter`, and `spring_summer` windows, count temperature frequencies by unique day using 1°C bins, and compute strict hot/cold groups from window-specific day-level temperature z-scores.
acceptance_criteria: A standalone backend helper returns `overall`, `fall_winter`, and `spring_summer` summary windows from canonical analytics dataset rows
Frequency bins count unique days rather than participant rows
`cold_group` uses `temperature_z < -2` and `hot_group` uses `temperature_z > 2` within each window
Empty seasonal windows or empty hot/cold groups serialize as zero-count payloads rather than errors
Regression coverage encodes the current workbook oracle counts and dates documented in `docs/labs/weather-wellness/ANALYTICS.md`
notes: Keep this task focused on the summary engine and its tests only; wiring into snapshot/live responses happens later.
If a new test file is created, keep fixture data minimal and deterministic.

tasks.title: Analytics v2 - Snapshot and route integration
tasks.summary: Wire the v2 temperature summary into backend snapshot/live responses and the same-origin analytics route/cache layer.
tasks.description: Integrate the new summary helper into the analytics service so `temperature_summary` appears in ready/stale/recomputing responses and round-trips through snapshot persistence. Update the same-origin Route Handler and cache key namespace to serve the v2 payload shape without mixing old cached snapshot data into new responses.
acceptance_criteria: `backend/app/services/analytics_service.py` includes `temperature_summary` in the returned analytics payload and persisted snapshot payloads
Snapshot and live responses continue to behave correctly for `ready`, `stale`, `recomputing`, `insufficient_data`, and `failed` states
The same-origin analytics route uses a v2 cache key namespace so old v1 cached payloads are not reused
Backend service/router tests and frontend route-handler tests are updated for the new field and version strings
notes: Do not add new dashboard UI in this task.
Preserve the existing snapshot-miss and background refresh behavior.

tasks.title: Analytics v2 - Minimal dashboard temperature summary UI
tasks.summary: Render a minimal RA-facing temperature summary card in the analytics section using the new v2 payload.
tasks.description: Add a compact dashboard UI for `temperature_summary` beneath the existing analytics cards/effect plot. Keep the initial UI simple: selector tabs for `overall`, `fall_winter`, and `spring_summer`; a small summary strip; a day-level frequency-vs-temperature chart or histogram; and hot/cold group panels listing counts and qualifying dates. Do not redesign the broader dashboard in this task.
acceptance_criteria: `DashboardAnalyticsSection` renders temperature summary controls for `overall`, `fall_winter`, and `spring_summer` when `temperature_summary` is present
The selected window shows day count, participant count, mean temperature, standard deviation, and hot/cold participant counts
The UI includes a simple day-level frequency-vs-temperature visualization based on `frequency_bins`
Hot and cold groups render qualifying dates and participant counts, and show a benign empty state when a group has no days
Existing model cards, status panels, and effect plot behavior remain intact
notes: Keep this as a first-pass UI; visual polish can follow in a later task.
Do not move or merge this summary into the weather time-series chart.

tasks.title: Dashboard filters - Latest study day metadata route and wrapper
tasks.summary: Expose the latest available `study_days.date_local` to the RA dashboard so preset ranges can anchor to actual study activity instead of wall-clock today.
tasks.description: Add a lightweight RA-only metadata read that returns `latest_study_day` as the maximum available `study_days.date_local`, or `null` when no study days exist yet. Surface it through a typed same-origin Next.js Route Handler and frontend API wrapper so dashboard surfaces can consume one shared anchor date without inferring it from weather or analytics payloads.
acceptance_criteria: A backend RA-only read returns `{ "latest_study_day": "YYYY-MM-DD | null" }` using the maximum available `study_days.date_local`
If no `study_days` rows exist yet, the backend returns `latest_study_day: null` rather than fabricating a date
A same-origin RA Route Handler proxies that payload for browser-owned reads
`frontend/src/lib/api/index.ts` exposes a typed wrapper for the same-origin metadata route
Backend and route-handler tests cover auth and the null/non-null response cases
notes: Keep this task metadata-only; do not refactor weather or analytics range controls yet.
The frontend fallback to Vancouver today belongs in later UI tasks, not in the backend response.

tasks.title: Dashboard filters - Weather card anchor-date refactor
tasks.summary: Refactor the weather card so its preset and custom date controls anchor to the latest study day while remaining independent from analytics refetches.
tasks.description: Update the dashboard page to load the new `latest_study_day` metadata and pass an anchor date into `WeatherUnifiedCard`. Refactor the weather card so `Study Start` / `Last 7` / `Last 30` / `Last 90` and the custom max-date input use `latest_study_day` when available and fall back to Vancouver today when it is null. Remove the weather card's `onDateRangeChange` callback so it no longer drives analytics state.
acceptance_criteria: The dashboard page reads `latest_study_day` once and derives a weather anchor date of `latest_study_day ?? today`
`WeatherUnifiedCard` preset handlers compute their end date from that anchor date instead of wall-clock today
The weather custom `To` input max date matches the same anchor date
When no study days exist, the weather card continues to function by falling back to Vancouver today
`WeatherUnifiedCard` no longer exposes or calls `onDateRangeChange`
notes: Keep the weather card's data fetching and ingest behavior intact.
Do not add analytics-side range controls in this task.

tasks.title: Dashboard filters - Independent analytics range controls
tasks.summary: Give the analytics card its own range state, presets, and custom filter controls anchored to the latest study day.
tasks.description: Refactor `DashboardAnalyticsSection` so it no longer receives a shared date range from the weather card. The analytics section should own internal range state initialized to `STUDY_START -> anchor_date`, render its own `Study Start` / `Last 7` / `Last 30` / `Last 90` / `Custom` controls, and use those dates for snapshot reads and background recompute requests. The temperature summary card remains inside the analytics surface and follows that same analytics range.
acceptance_criteria: `DashboardAnalyticsSection` owns internal `dateFrom` / `dateTo` state initialized from `study_start` and the provided anchor date
The analytics card renders preset buttons and custom date controls separate from the weather card
Changing analytics filters re-fetches only analytics data for the selected analytics range
Manual analytics refresh uses the analytics card's active range and keeps the previous snapshot visible while recompute is in progress
The temperature summary card follows the analytics range instead of any weather-card range
notes: Keep the analytics snapshot/live route contract unchanged in this task.
Do not remove weather-chart linkage props until the independent analytics controls are in place.

tasks.title: Dashboard filters - Remove weather analytics linkage cleanup
tasks.summary: Clean up the transitional weather-to-analytics linkage UI once the weather and analytics ranges are independent.
tasks.description: Remove the dashboard's `analyticsAnnotation` plumbing and the weather chart's analysis badge/plot-band overlay so the decoupled weather and analytics cards do not imply a hidden shared range. Keep the effect plot and temperature summary inside the analytics section and leave backend visualization serialization untouched unless a small cleanup is required for dead code removal.
acceptance_criteria: The dashboard page no longer manages `analyticsAnnotation` state
`WeatherUnifiedCard` no longer accepts or renders analytics-linked badge/plot-band UI
The analytics section no longer emits cross-card annotation callbacks solely for weather-chart overlays
Effect plot and temperature summary behavior inside the analytics surface remain intact
Regression coverage is updated so the removed linkage UI is not expected anymore
notes: This is a cleanup task after the analytics range split lands.
Do not remove backend `visualizations.weather_annotations` unless the tests and docs are updated together.

tasks.title: Dashboard polish - Standalone temperature summary card with dedicated compute action
tasks.summary: Move the day-level temperature summary into its own always-visible dashboard card and give it a dedicated compute/recompute control.
tasks.description: Refactor the RA dashboard so the day-level temperature summary is no longer buried inside `DashboardAnalyticsSection`. Render it as its own card-level surface on the dashboard, keep the shell visible even when no analytics snapshot exists yet, and add a dedicated compute/recompute button for that card instead of relying on the analytics section's general refresh button. Preserve the existing summary windows and analytics-backed payload shape unless a small shared-loader refactor is required.
acceptance_criteria: The dashboard renders a standalone temperature summary card outside the current analytics section
The temperature summary card remains visible when there is no analytics snapshot or no summary payload yet
The card exposes its own dedicated compute/recompute button with clear RA-facing empty/loading/error states
The existing `overall`, `fall_winter`, and `spring_summer` summary windows still work once summary data is available
The analytics section no longer needs to be populated before an RA can see that the temperature summary surface exists
notes: Keep this as a frontend behavior/layout task unless a narrowly scoped shared loader change is needed.
Do not merge the temperature summary into the weather Highcharts card.

tasks.title: Dashboard polish - Weather chart defaults to temperature only
tasks.summary: Simplify the weather chart's default presentation so only the temperature series is enabled initially.
tasks.description: Update `WeatherUnifiedCard` so the Highcharts card initially shows only the temperature series as selected, while keeping precipitation, forecast high, and forecast low available as optional toggles. The goal is to reduce first-load clutter without removing the other series from the card.
acceptance_criteria: On first render, only the temperature series is visibly enabled in the weather chart
Other supported weather series remain available through the existing toggle controls
Changing presets or custom date ranges does not automatically turn the other series back on
The weather chart still renders a valid empty/loading state when no range data is available
notes: Keep all current weather series available; this task changes only the default selection state.

tasks.title: Dashboard polish - RA-focused analytics section cleanup
tasks.summary: Trim developer-centric copy and metadata from the analytics section so it reads as a cleaner RA-facing surface.
tasks.description: Audit the current `DashboardAnalyticsSection` UI from an RA's perspective and remove, demote, or simplify text that is mostly useful to the developer rather than a lab member. Keep the controls and information an RA actually needs to understand the date range, whether analytics are available, the main model outputs, and how to trigger a recompute; reduce or hide status jargon, cache language, internal snapshot lifecycle timestamps, and other low-signal clutter unless they directly help an RA make a decision.
acceptance_criteria: The analytics section retains clear RA-usable controls for date range selection and recompute
Developer-centric labels and metadata are removed, shortened, or hidden behind lightweight progressive disclosure when they are not useful to an RA
The section still communicates meaningful empty/loading/error states without relying on internal implementation jargon
The visible copy is materially shorter and less cluttered than the current version while preserving important analytics outputs
notes: Make judgement calls in favor of RA clarity over developer diagnostics.
If technical diagnostics still matter, prefer moving them into a collapsed details affordance rather than the default view.

tasks.title: Temperature summary repair - Backend end-to-end payload wiring
tasks.summary: Wire the documented day-level temperature summary helper into the analytics modeling/service pipeline so non-empty summary data actually reaches snapshot/live responses.
tasks.description: The standalone `build_temperature_summary()` helper exists and has isolated regression tests, but the analytics modeling/service path does not currently carry `temperature_summary` through to the serialized dashboard response and persisted snapshots. Integrate the helper into the canonical analytics response path so snapshot, stale, and recomputing reads preserve the most recent computed summary payload instead of falling back to the schema default empty object. The current `backend/tests/test_analytics_service.py` suite also hangs in this environment during a success-path orchestration test; fix that backend test-path issue as part of this task so the service-level regression suite is usable as the verification gate for the new wiring.
acceptance_criteria: `build_temperature_summary()` is invoked from the canonical analytics response pipeline for the active dataset window
Ready analytics responses include the computed `temperature_summary` payload rather than the schema default empty object
Persisted snapshot payloads preserve the computed `temperature_summary` and snapshot/live reads round-trip it correctly
When a prior snapshot exists, stale/recomputing responses continue to surface that prior computed summary payload
Backend regression coverage fails if `temperature_summary` is accidentally dropped from future analytics responses
The targeted backend analytics service/router regression suite completes successfully under pytest in this repo environment
notes: Keep the summary descriptive-only; do not change mixed-model formulas or sampling rules.
Prefer adding integration coverage over expanding the isolated helper tests.
If the existing analytics-service regression suite needs a small repair to stop hanging, treat that as in-scope for this task rather than creating a separate follow-up.
commands: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_analytics_temperature_summary.py tests/test_analytics_service.py tests/test_dashboard_analytics_router.py

tasks.title: Temperature summary repair - Standalone section range, state, and recompute UX
tasks.summary: Make the standalone temperature summary section behave as its own end-to-end surface with an independent date range, accurate ready/empty states, and background recompute handling.
tasks.description: Refactor `AnalyticsTemperatureSummaryCard` so it no longer hard-codes only `study_start -> anchor_date`. The section should own its own preset/custom date range, issue summary reads and recompute requests for that active summary window, treat an empty default payload distinctly from a real saved summary, and keep the last available summary visible while the backend reports `recomputing` until a follow-up snapshot read returns the finished payload.
acceptance_criteria: The standalone temperature summary section owns internal `dateFrom` / `dateTo` state with preset and custom controls independent from both weather and mixed-model analytics
Compute/recompute actions use the section's active summary range rather than a fixed `study_start -> anchor_date` range
The section distinguishes a real saved summary from an empty default payload and does not label `{ windows: [] }` as ready
When live recompute returns `recomputing`, the section keeps the previous summary visible and polls snapshot mode until the refreshed summary is available or an error state is reached
Frontend regression coverage exercises independent range changes plus ready / missing-snapshot / recomputing summary states
notes: Keep the temperature summary section standalone; do not re-couple it to `DashboardAnalyticsSection`.
Reuse the existing same-origin analytics handler unless backend contract changes become unavoidable.

tasks.title: Temperature summary chart - Threshold contract and backend payload wiring
tasks.summary: Extend the temperature summary contract so each window exposes the descriptive cold/hot threshold temperatures implied by the existing day-level z-score rule.
tasks.description: Update the temperature summary schema and backend helper so each summary window serializes `cold_threshold_temperature_c`, `hot_threshold_temperature_c`, `threshold_method`, and `threshold_z_cutoff` alongside the existing mean, SD, bins, and hot/cold groups. The new fields must stay aligned with the current window-specific day-level z-score method used for `temperature_z < -2` / `temperature_z > 2`, return `null` thresholds when the window has fewer than two unique days or zero variance, and round-trip through snapshot/live analytics responses without changing mixed-model formulas or extreme-day membership.
acceptance_criteria: Each populated `temperature_summary.windows[]` item includes `cold_threshold_temperature_c`, `hot_threshold_temperature_c`, `threshold_method`, and `threshold_z_cutoff` in the backend schema and frontend typed response models
Threshold temperatures are computed from the selected window's descriptive day-level summary stats using `mean_temperature_c ± (2 * sd_temperature_c)`
`threshold_method` serializes as `window_day_zscore_v1` and `threshold_z_cutoff` serializes as `2`
Threshold temperatures are `null` when the window has fewer than 2 unique days or zero day-level temperature variance
Existing `cold_group` / `hot_group` membership remains strict (`temperature_z < -2`, `temperature_z > 2`) and regression coverage fails if that behavior changes
Snapshot/live analytics reads preserve the new threshold fields end to end
notes: Keep the new threshold fields descriptive-only; do not introduce a new inferential significance method in this task.
Do not recompute alternative threshold methods or change the existing hot/cold grouping rule.
commands: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_analytics_temperature_summary.py tests/test_analytics_service.py tests/test_dashboard_analytics_router.py

tasks.title: Temperature summary chart - Conclusive Highcharts histogram with threshold overlays
tasks.summary: Replace the current temperature-bin grid with one Highcharts histogram per active summary window, including mean and descriptive threshold overlays.
tasks.description: Refactor `AnalyticsTemperatureSummaryCard` so the selected window renders a single Highcharts column histogram from `frequency_bins` instead of the current CSS bar grid. The chart should stay scoped to the active summary tab, overlay the selected window's mean temperature and descriptive cold/hot thresholds from the analytics payload, include concise RA-facing explainer copy about the threshold method, and gracefully fall back to frequency-plus-mean only when threshold temperatures are unavailable. Preserve the card's existing standalone range controls, compute/recompute behavior, empty states, and hot/cold day panels.
acceptance_criteria: The standalone temperature summary card renders one Highcharts histogram for the active window using the existing `frequency_bins` payload
The chart overlays the selected window's mean temperature plus cold/hot threshold markers sourced from the serialized threshold fields rather than recomputed alternate logic
When threshold temperatures are `null`, the chart omits threshold overlays and shows a concise explanatory fallback message instead of failing
The chart remains scoped to the selected summary tab and updates correctly when the user switches between `overall`, `fall_winter`, and `spring_summer`
The card keeps the existing summary stats, hot/cold day panels, and standalone compute/recompute UX intact
Frontend regression coverage and build checks pass with the new Highcharts temperature summary visualization
notes: Keep the temperature summary card separate from `WeatherUnifiedCard`; do not merge this histogram into the weather time-series chart.
Follow the repo Highcharts conventions for mounted rendering, CSS-variable theming, and declarative chart options.

tasks.title: Legacy import authority - Reconcile imported-only rows against the authoritative workbook
tasks.summary: Add a dry-run/apply reconciliation path that removes imported-only DB rows no longer present in `reference/data_complete.xlsx` while protecting native data.
tasks.description: Implement a backend reconciliation CLI/service that compares the current imported participant/session graph in the database against the authoritative workbook `reference/data_complete.xlsx`. The tool should report missing workbook participant IDs and shifted-date cases in dry-run mode, then optionally delete only imported-only participant/session/outcome rows that are absent from the workbook. Native rows must remain a hard stop, not a delete target. This task closes the full-authority import gap left by the upsert-only commit path.
acceptance_criteria: A backend CLI supports both dry-run and apply modes for authoritative workbook reconciliation
Dry-run output identifies imported-only participants/sessions present in the DB but absent from `reference/data_complete.xlsx`
Apply mode deletes only imported-only rows and refuses to remove any participant/session graph that has native survey or digit span data
The current known old-only imported participant (`142`) is removed by the reconciliation flow when present and still workbook-absent
Regression coverage verifies that imported-only rows are deleted and native rows are protected
Operational docs describe when to run reconciliation after an authoritative workbook refresh
notes: Prefer a dedicated reconciliation entrypoint rather than folding delete behavior into the normal import commit path.
The reconciliation tool should emit a machine-readable summary so operators can archive the authority diff.

tasks.title: Legacy import verification - Workbook z-score and overlap cross-check CLI
tasks.summary: Build a read-only verification CLI that compares the authoritative workbook against current import semantics and documents expected z-score parity versus divergence.
tasks.description: Add a backend verification script that reads `reference/data_complete.xlsx` and optionally `reference/data_full_1-230.xlsx`, normalizes both to the import schema, reports added/shifted/removed participant-date keys, and recomputes workbook z-scores for comparison. Participant-level `_z` columns (`anxiety_z`, `depression_z`, `loneliness_z`, `self_report_z`, `digit_span_z`) should be checked for exact row-level sample-z parity. Weather `_z` columns should be reported as informational because the backend analytics pipeline intentionally uses unique-day weather standardization rather than workbook row-level weather z-scores.
acceptance_criteria: The verification CLI is read-only and does not require schema changes or DB writes
The CLI reports key-level overlap/add/remove/shift differences between `data_complete.xlsx` and `data_full_1-230.xlsx` after canonical column normalization
Participant-level workbook `_z` columns are recomputed and verified against exact row-level sample z-scores
Weather `_z` columns are reported with an explicit informational note that workbook row-level z-scores are not expected to match backend unique-day weather z-score semantics
The output is concise enough for operator review and stable enough for future regression checks
Regression coverage validates the normalization and participant-level z-score parity logic
notes: Keep workbook `_z` columns as a verification surface only; do not feed them into runtime analytics.
The CLI should call out expected weather-z divergence as informational, not as a hard failure.

tasks.title: Analytics v2 - Repair visualization contract regression coverage
tasks.summary: Restore a green backend analytics regression suite by fixing the visualization-contract tests to respect the day-level weather uniqueness invariant.
tasks.description: Investigate the current failures in `backend/tests/test_analytics_visualization_contract.py`, where the varied fixture produces conflicting per-day temperatures that violate the temperature-summary contract. Update the fixture and/or supporting helper expectations so visualization-contract tests exercise the intended API shape without fabricating multiple weather values for a single `date_local`. Keep the MLM formulas, import semantics, and backend visualization payload structure unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: `backend/tests/test_analytics_visualization_contract.py` passes again under the current analytics v2 contract
The full backend analytics regression suite passes after the fix
No changes are made to mixed-model formulas, imported workbook handling, or the separation between effect plots and weather annotations
If fixture data changes are required, each `date_local` in the visualization-contract fixtures has exactly one weather value per day
Docs remain aligned with the existing visualization contract and temperature-summary semantics
notes: The current failures were reproduced after the authoritative import refresh and are test-layer regressions, not evidence that the live import path broke.
Prefer a fixture correction over broadening the production contract unless a real backend bug is confirmed.
commands: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_analytics_dataset.py tests/test_analytics_modeling.py tests/test_analytics_parity.py tests/test_analytics_schema.py tests/test_analytics_service.py tests/test_dashboard_analytics_router.py tests/test_analytics_temperature_summary.py tests/test_analytics_visualization_contract.py tests/test_analytics_storage_models.py

tasks.title: Temperature summary drilldown - Backend bin participant-session contract
tasks.summary: Extend histogram bins with participant-session metadata so the temperature summary chart can support hover drilldown without changing its day-level counting semantics.
tasks.description: Update the analytics dataset and temperature summary backend contract so each `temperature_summary.windows[].frequency_bins[]` item carries additive `participant_sessions[]` metadata for the participant-session rows that fall into that 1°C bin. Each row should include `participant_uuid`, RA-facing `participant_number`, `session_id`, and `date_local`. Preserve existing `day_count` behavior as a unique-day measure, keep hot/cold grouping logic unchanged, and round-trip the expanded payload through snapshot/live analytics responses.
acceptance_criteria: Each populated `frequency_bins[]` item includes `participant_sessions[]` in the backend analytics schema and serialized dashboard analytics response
Each `participant_sessions[]` entry includes `participant_uuid`, `participant_number`, `session_id`, and `date_local`
Bin `day_count` remains a day-level count and does not change to participant-row counting
Participant-session rows are assigned to the same 1°C floor-based binning rule already used for `day_count` aggregation
Existing hot/cold groups, threshold fields, and overall temperature-summary window stats remain unchanged
Backend regression coverage verifies the new bin metadata and snapshot/live response serialization
notes: Treat `participant_sessions[]` as additive drilldown metadata only; do not redefine the histogram as participant-row frequency.
Use RA-facing `participant_number` for drilldown display support, but keep `participant_uuid` and `session_id` in the payload for stable lookup keys.
commands: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_analytics_temperature_summary.py tests/test_analytics_service.py tests/test_dashboard_analytics_router.py

tasks.title: Temperature summary drilldown - Same-origin participant demographics read
tasks.summary: Add the RA-facing same-origin participant demographics read needed by the histogram drilldown panel and align the frontend participant types with the backend schema.
tasks.description: Create a same-origin RA route handler for `GET /api/ra/participants/[participantUuid]` that proxies the existing backend participant read, expand the frontend `ParticipantResponse` typing so it includes the demographic/exposure fields already returned by the backend, and add a typed frontend helper for loading participant demographics from the dashboard. Keep this task scoped to participant demographics only; do not add session timing or score aggregation endpoints here.
acceptance_criteria: A same-origin RA route exists for `GET /api/ra/participants/[participantUuid]` and proxies the backend participant read
Frontend `ParticipantResponse` typing includes the demographic/exposure fields already present in the backend participant schema
A typed frontend helper loads participant demographics from the same-origin route for dashboard use
RA route-topology coverage is updated for the new participant route and any new route test coverage passes
No new backend aggregation endpoint for session timing or scores is introduced in this task
notes: Reuse the existing backend `/participants/{participant_uuid}` endpoint rather than creating a dashboard-specific backend route.
Keep the response demographics-only in this first pass; session timing and score drilldown are deferred.

tasks.title: Temperature summary drilldown - Histogram hover list and pinned demographics panel
tasks.summary: Use Highcharts point events to drive a React hover list for each histogram bin and a pinned participant demographics panel.
tasks.description: Refactor `AnalyticsTemperatureSummaryCard` so hovering or tapping a histogram bin reveals a React-rendered hover card listing that bin's `participant_sessions[]` rows as `Participant #<number> · <date>`. Clicking a row should load participant demographics through the same-origin helper and render a pinned detail panel next to the chart on desktop and below it on mobile. Keep the current summary stats, threshold overlays, compute/recompute flow, and hot/cold day panels intact, and do not implement session timing or score detail in this first version.
acceptance_criteria: Hovering a histogram bin reveals the participant-session rows for that bin using the new `participant_sessions[]` payload
Each interactive row displays `Participant #<participant_number> · <date_local>` and is clickable
The hover surface remains usable while the pointer stays over the active bar or the hover card itself, with a touch-friendly tap path on mobile
Clicking a row loads and renders a pinned participant demographics panel, side-by-side on desktop and stacked below on smaller screens
The implementation uses Highcharts point events plus React state rather than relying on a clickable native chart tooltip
Frontend regression coverage and build checks pass with the new histogram drilldown UI
notes: Render the interactive hover card in React outside the native Highcharts tooltip.
Keep the first version demographics-only; do not add session timing or score breakdowns in this task.
commands: cd frontend && npm test -- src/lib/analytics/dashboard-analytics-loader.test.ts src/lib/analytics/ui-utils.test.ts src/app/api/ra/route-topology.test.ts

tasks.title: Trial run mode - Shared frontend mode plumbing and participant watermark
tasks.summary: Introduce a shared frontend trial-mode signal and a reusable centered top-screen Trial Run watermark for participant flows.
tasks.description: Add a frontend-only trial-mode plumbing layer that can be toggled from RA launch pages and read consistently by participant flow pages. The shared layer should define fake-id generation helpers, mode propagation strategy, and a reusable UI primitive that renders a centered top-screen 'Trial Run' watermark whenever trial mode is active. Keep this task strictly to shared mode/watermark infrastructure and avoid wiring specific WW or Misokinesia submit behavior here.
acceptance_criteria: A shared trial-mode mechanism exists that can be set on RA launch pages and consumed by participant flow pages
Frontend helpers generate clearly fake local-only ids for trial mode without colliding with backend-issued ids
A reusable participant overlay/watermark component renders centered at the top of the screen with the text 'Trial Run'
The shared trial-mode plumbing is documented in code comments/types well enough for WW and Misokinesia flows to consume without duplicate state logic
notes: Use frontend-local state only; do not add backend endpoints or schema changes.
Do not start this task until T142 Storybook mocks are reviewed and approved.
Preserve existing production flow behavior while adding trial-mode support.

tasks.title: Trial run mode - WW launch and survey plus digitspan local submit flow
tasks.summary: Add a Run Test Trial option to the WW launch path and run the full survey plus digitspan participant flow with local-only submit progression.
tasks.description: Update the `/new-session` flow so Step 2 offers both production start and Run Test Trial after consent is shown. In trial mode, navigate into the existing WW participant pages with fake session identifiers, keep screen order unchanged, advance each survey via local simulated success, and complete Digit Span with local simulated submit plus completion transition. Ensure production mode still calls the typed API wrappers exactly as before.
acceptance_criteria: The `/new-session` demographics step exposes a Run Test Trial action alongside the production start action
Trial mode keeps consent visible and runs the full WW participant flow end-to-end to completion
In trial mode, survey and digitspan submit handlers do not call backend endpoints
Run Test Trial launch controls match the approved Storybook mock
The Trial Run watermark is visible on WW participant trial-mode screens
Production WW mode remains unchanged and still posts to canonical FastAPI endpoints
notes: Do not skip the consent view in trial mode.
Trial-mode fake ids should be obvious non-production values for debugging.

tasks.title: Trial run mode - Misokinesia launch and local questionnaire progression
tasks.summary: Add a Run Test Trial option to Misokinesia launch and execute the participant state machine locally without backend calls.
tasks.description: Extend the `/misokinesia` launch experience so RAs can choose Run Test Trial. In trial mode, construct a local fake manifest and ids, run intro to clip playback to questionnaire loop and end-of-task flow, and finalize at completion without calling backend start/response/end-of-task endpoints. Keep the current production Misokinesia launch and submit behavior unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: The `/misokinesia` launch page includes a Run Test Trial option in addition to Start Misokinesia Session
Trial mode runs the full Misokinesia participant flow to completion using local fake manifest data
No Misokinesia backend endpoints are called in trial mode (`/misokinesia/start`, `/responses`, `/end-of-task`)
Run Test Trial launch controls match the approved Storybook mock
The Trial Run watermark is visible throughout Misokinesia participant trial-mode screens
Production Misokinesia mode remains unchanged and still uses canonical backend calls
notes: Superseded by T143/T144: Misokinesia trial clips should come from a read-only 5-video Supabase Storage manifest rather than deterministic local placeholders.
Do not alter seeded-stimulus backend behavior in this task.

tasks.title: Trial run mode - Storybook launch-button mocks for WW and Misokinesia
tasks.summary: Draft Storybook mockups for the new Run Test Trial launch controls on both RA pages before wiring the real UI.
tasks.description: Add or update Storybook page-replica stories so `/new-session` and `/misokinesia` each show the proposed launch-control layout with both production start and Run Test Trial actions. Keep this task mock-only: no route implementation changes to production pages. Publish the mock state variants needed for developer review, then stop for manual inspection.
acceptance_criteria: Storybook contains a mock for WW `/new-session` launch controls showing both Start Session and Run Test Trial actions
Storybook contains a mock for Misokinesia launch controls showing both Start Misokinesia Session and Run Test Trial actions
Mocks include Replica plus Dark and Mobile review states for both pages
No production route behavior is changed as part of this task
Task output is ready for manual project-owner inspection before T139/T140 proceed
notes: Manual gate token for downstream implementation tasks: `MANUAL_TRIAL_RUN_MOCK_APPROVAL`.
After review approval, clear that token from `blocked_by` on T138.

tasks.title: Trial run mode - Frontend regression coverage for no-backend rehearsal paths
tasks.summary: Add focused frontend tests proving trial mode avoids API calls and still reaches completion across both WW and Misokinesia flows.
tasks.description: Add or extend frontend test coverage for trial-mode branching logic and launch controls, including assertions that trial mode does not invoke typed API wrappers for participant submissions while production mode continues to do so. Include checks for Trial Run watermark visibility in trial-mode states and keep test scope to utility/state transitions and route-handler topology-friendly surfaces supported by the current Node test environment.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend tests assert Run Test Trial launch controls exist for WW and Misokinesia launch paths
Trial-mode branch tests verify no API wrapper calls occur for survey, digitspan, or misokinesia submissions
Production-mode branch tests verify canonical API wrapper calls remain intact
Tests cover Trial Run watermark visibility for trial-mode participant screens
Frontend test suite and build pass after adding trial-mode coverage
notes: Current frontend tests run in Node and do not render React components directly.
If component rendering tests are required, include the necessary test-environment update in the same task.

tasks.title: Trial run mode - Read-only Misokinesia trial manifest endpoint
tasks.summary: Add a no-write RA endpoint that returns 5 randomly sampled active Misokinesia clip URLs for test trials.
tasks.description: Implement the planned `GET /misokinesia/trial-manifest` endpoint. The endpoint must resolve the active Misokinesia test set, sample exactly 5 active stimuli by `stimulus_id` on each request, and return public Supabase Storage CDN URLs plus clip metadata without creating or updating any participant, session, misokinesia_participant, or response rows. Keep production `POST /misokinesia/start` behavior unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: `GET /misokinesia/trial-manifest` requires RA auth and returns 401 for unauthenticated requests
The endpoint returns exactly 5 active clips from the active Misokinesia test set when at least 5 active stimuli exist
Repeated calls can return different stimulus subsets and/or orders because sampling is randomized by `stimulus_id` membership/order
Each returned clip includes `stimulus_id`, public Supabase Storage `public_url`, `sort_order`, and `duration_ms`
The endpoint performs no database writes and does not create `participants`, `sessions`, `misokinesia_participants`, or response rows
If fewer than 5 active stimuli are available, the endpoint returns a clear non-2xx error instead of silently shortening the trial
Production `POST /misokinesia/start` still returns all 29 active clips and still creates the canonical rows
notes: Do not add schema migrations for this task.
Do not proxy video bytes through FastAPI; only return public Supabase Storage URLs.
This task supersedes the previous local-placeholder trial clip behavior for Misokinesia test trials.

tasks.title: Trial run mode - Misokinesia real 5-video rehearsal playback
tasks.summary: Update Misokinesia Run Test Trial to fetch the read-only 5-clip manifest and play real Supabase-hosted videos while keeping submissions local.
tasks.description: Replace the current local fake 29-clip Misokinesia trial manifest and placeholder playback with the new read-only trial manifest flow. On each `Run Test Trial` click, call the typed wrapper for `GET /misokinesia/trial-manifest`, combine the returned 5 clips with frontend fake ids, persist the trial manifest in session storage, and navigate to the participant task with `?trial=1`. The participant task should play the sampled public video URLs with the real Misokinesia video player, loop through only those 5 clips, then run the end-of-task and completion transitions locally without write API calls.
acceptance_criteria: Misokinesia `Run Test Trial` calls only the read-only trial manifest API before navigation and does not call `/misokinesia/start`
The trial manifest stored in session storage contains fake trial ids plus exactly 5 real clip records returned by the backend
Trial playback uses the same video player path as production and loads each sampled clip from its public Supabase Storage URL
The participant task shows `Clip X of 5` in Misokinesia trial mode and reaches end-of-task after the fifth sampled clip
Per-clip questionnaire, end-of-task, and final session completion transitions remain local-only in trial mode and do not call write endpoints
Production Misokinesia launch and 29-clip participant flow remain unchanged
Launch errors from the read-only manifest request are visible to the RA and do not navigate into a broken trial
notes: Keep fake ids clearly non-production; only clip metadata and video URLs should come from backend/storage.
Do not reintroduce local `trial://` clip URLs or placeholder playback for the happy path.
All frontend API calls must go through typed wrappers in `src/lib/api/`.
